examples: Tumorigenesis is cogently associated with the activation of telomerase.
Most cogently, the presence of the metabolic syndrome is also a strong predictor of future development of NAFLD.
Compared with other learning theories of the past, conceptual change theory seems to more cogently set forth the issue of learning mechanism.
An agency law conception of the board-officer relationship cogently highlights how bewildering and unhealthy this practice really is.
Wolf argues, rather cogently, that the answer is to increase exports and aim for a further weakening of the pound.
